free_text: Fission fragment yields in the fission of Th-232 by
Thorium targets were about 500 mu-g/cm2 thick and
prepared by electrodeposition onto a 4 mg/cm**2 thick
aluminum backing foil from an isopropanol solution at
900 Volts. The thorium targets were wrapped with
aluminum cover foils of 8 mg/cm2 thickness for complete
collection of fission products. Five to fifteen of the
wrapped targets were then stacked for irradiation.
As a proton-energy monitor 3.12 mg/cm2 thick silver
foils and 10.1 mg/cm2 thick copper foils were inserted
into the target stack at appropriate places.
40 cm3 Ge(Li) detector equipped with a
2048 pulse height analyzer. The energy resolution of
the Ge(Li) detector system was 2.3 keV (FWHM) for the
The amount of Th-232 was determined by alpha-
spectrometry with a surface barrier detector of known
The measured gamma-ray activities were corrected for:
* and the target amount to give excitation functions.
Data are not corrected for the effect of neutron
